era_context: Continuation of the early Iron Age. Sargon II is in the early years of his reign in Assyria. Greece remains in the Geometric period. The Zhou Dynasty continues in China. Egypt is under the 22nd Dynasty (Libyan rulers). The Mediterranean is characterized by increasing trade and the rise of Phoenician commercial networks.
geographic_coverage_gaps: Greece (limited specific events for this year)
China (Zhou Dynasty records are fragmentary for this period)

events.title: Sargon II's consolidation of Assyrian power
events.description: In the early years of his reign, Sargon II worked to consolidate power within Assyria itself, suppressing internal dissent and reorganizing the military and administrative apparatus. While specific events for 771 BCE are not individually documented, this period represents the foundation-building phase of his later expansionist campaigns. The reorganization of the Assyrian state under Sargon II set the stage for the empire's greatest territorial extent.

sources.name: Sargon II's annals (later compilations and inscriptions)

events.certainty_note: Sargon II's reign is well-attested overall, but specific events within individual years are rarely documented. This entry reflects the general context of his early reign rather than a specific dated event.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific events are reliably attested for 771 BCE. The Assyrian annals group events by campaign or reign period rather than by individual year, making precise year-by-year attribution speculative. This year may be historically significant only as part of the broader context of Sargon II's reign.
historiographic_note: 771 BCE falls in a period where written documentation is sparse outside of Mesopotamia and Egypt. The Assyrian record is the most detailed, but even it does not provide year-by-year granularity for administrative or internal events. Greek history is almost entirely dependent on archaeology, which rarely provides precise annual dating. Chinese records from the Zhou Dynasty exist but are often legendary or semi-legendary for this period. The historical record for this year is thin and heavily weighted toward the Near East.

graph_edges.note: Part of the same period of Sargon II's reign
